text.text:
I had high hopes for this restaurant going in because of the great reviews and a push for reservations. I will say immediately Yunomi is an experience best for two person groups. There is only bar seating, so eating with a larger 4 person group meant it was difficult to have conversations all together. Due to its popularity, there was a 45 minute time limit on our meal which meant everything felt very rushed. I ordered a pot of hot tea, but because of the time limitations, even though a 2nd brew is offered for free, I was unable to partake in it and rushed to finish even my first pot. As the shrimp tempura didn't appeal to me on the set menus, I opted to order each handroll individually. The tuna was great, the yellowtail just okay. I have an issue with the scallop as no where on the menu did it state it comes with a very heavy and creamy sauce. This drowned out the flavor of the scallop, and I was very disappointed. This needs to be stated as a subtitle on the menu as I was expecting the scallop to come by itself and if I had known prior, I would not have ordered it to begin with. I really enjoyed the soft shell crab although both it and the the unagi roll were VERY greasy. You are given a hand wipe before you start eating which is soon after taken away. The napkin provided did not help with the grease at all. Alongside the rice, fish, and sauce that ended up on my fingers after multiple handrolls, my hands felt very sticky at the end. I had to ask for another wet towel to clean them. Each fish handroll apart from the cucumber and avocado which are priced $4.50 each and $5.50 together, the prices range from $6.50 to $11. Because of all of this, I do not believe it's worth the price. If you do go for the experience, get one handroll and then eat your fill elsewhere.

The photos: Menu, Tuna Handroll, Soft Shell Crab Handroll, Unagi Handroll.

text.text:
Visited Yunomi Handroll recently with my wife, and we really enjoyed trying something new. It feels like handroll spots are becoming a big trend in downtown LA these days, and this place is definitely part of that wave. Yunomi has even received recognition from the Michelin Guide, so we were pretty excited to check it out.

When we arrived, the place was already packed and people were waiting for seats. The setup is bar-style dining, and the rolls are made right in front of you, which is pretty fun. The flavor was good overall—fresh, clean, and a bit unique in its own way—but maybe because I had high expectations from the Michelin feature, it didn’t quite exceed what I imagined. Still, everything was solid and enjoyable.

Like many spots in downtown LA, the prices felt a bit on the higher side. Also, parking in this area is tough. They do offer valet, but it’s not cheap. If you plan ahead and find street parking early, you can save a bit.

Overall, good food, trendy vibe, and a nice experience trying something new—just be prepared for parking challenges and slightly higher prices.

text.text:
I heard good things about Yunomi and I was looking for a new restaurant to try with a friend and we came here! We got the 3-roll combo because we are both broke college students and felt like this was a good combination that could allow us to sample some of the options.

The tuna was fairly unmemorable but the quality of the fish was solid. The salmon was buttery and had a very clean bite, which was probably my favorite roll. I liked the crispiness of the tempura and the contrast with the cucumber, but it was a little batter heavy.

Overall, it was a little pricey for how much food it was but 3 rolls was sufficiently filling for me.

text.text:
January 2026 - Made a reservation. Since there's a time limit on the seating and wait times can be long, I highly recommend making a reservation. Overall I would come back if I feel like dealing with the parking situation.

Food/Pricing - Spicy Albacore Crispy Rice $16 - if you do not enjoy fried onion, this dish may not be for you as they do add hella onion on top of the albacore but I do enjoy the taste of it so I loved this dish.

Six Handroll Set $30 - tuna, albacore, salmon, yellowtail, scallop, shrimp tempura. All solid and we ordered all of them spicy, which honestly was not spicy at all but just had a very slight kick.

Service/Ambiance - quick and friendly service.

Parking - Paid a crazy amount to park across the street. The rate was like $4 for every 15 minutes. Try your best to find street parking.

text.text:
No-frills handroll experience that focuses on the essentials. The handrolls are solid across the board, but the standout here is actually the spicy albacore crispy rice. The albacore is soft, enhanced by the truffle soy sauce. But it’s the crunchy onions, generously piled on top, that enhance it further. I was even using them to garnish my handrolls. The wasabi peas were the right temperature of warm, an addictive snack to munch on before the handrolls.

The tuna, albacore, salmon, shrimp tempura, and yellowtail handrolls all consist of fresh fish and fairly crisp seaweed. However, the rice-to-fish ratio felt a little off for me. The rolls were substantial, but I did feel the rice could be toned down a bit. Still, the quality of the ingredients, paired with the efficient sushi bar service, made for a satisfying meal. Good value for the price. The space is light and airy, the right mix of casual and contemporary for a quick handroll fix.
